statement.title: Statement
statement.content:
Description
You are given an array of positive integers $ a_1,a_2,\ldots,a_n $ of length $ n $ .

In one operation you can jump from index $ i $ to index $ j $ ( $ 1 \le i \le j \le n $ ) by paying $ \min(a_i, a_{i + 1}, \ldots, a_j) \cdot (j - i)^2 $ eris.

For all $ k $ from $ 1 $ to $ n $ , find the minimum number of eris needed to get from index $ 1 $ to index $ k $ .

Input Format
The first line contains a single integer $ n $ ( $ 2 \le n \le 4 \cdot 10^5 $ ).

The second line contains $ n $ integers $ a_1,a_2,\ldots a_n $ ( $ 1 \le a_i \le n $ ).

Output Format
Output $ n $ integers — the $ k $ -th integer is the minimum number of eris needed to reach index $ k $ if you start from index $ 1 $ .

Examples
Sample 1
Input
3
2 1 3
Output
0 1 2

Sample 2
Input
6
1 4 1 6 3 2
Output
0 1 2 3 6 8

Sample 3
Input
2
1 2
Output
0 1

Sample 4
Input
4
1 4 4 4
Output
0 1 4 8

Note
In the first example:

- From $ 1 $ to $ 1 $ : the cost is $ 0 $ ,
- From $ 1 $ to $ 2 $ : $ 1 \rightarrow 2 $ — the cost is $ \min(2, 1) \cdot (2 - 1) ^ 2=1 $ ,
- From $ 1 $ to $ 3 $ : $ 1 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 3 $ — the cost is $ \min(2, 1) \cdot (2 - 1) ^ 2 + \min(1, 3) \cdot (3 - 2) ^ 2 = 1 + 1 = 2 $ .

In the fourth example from $ 1 $ to $ 4 $ : $ 1 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 4 $ — the cost is $ \min(1, 4, 4) \cdot (3 - 1) ^ 2 + \min(4, 4) \cdot (4 - 3) ^ 2 = 4 + 4 = 8 $ .

hints.title: Hints
hints.content:
Hint 0
Use dynamic programming.
Hint 1
$$$a_i \le n$$$
Hint 2
Compare $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) \cdot (j - i)^2$$$ with $$$n \cdot (j - i)$$$.
Hint 3
Compare $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) \cdot (j - i)^2$$$ with $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_k) \cdot (k - i)^2 + \min(a_k \ldots a_j) \cdot (j - k)^2$$$ for some $$$i \lt k \lt j$$$.
Hint 4
Two cases: $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) \ge \sqrt{n}$$$ and $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) \lt \sqrt{n}$$$.
Hint 5
Split $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) \lt \sqrt{n}$$$ into two more cases: $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) = a_i$$$ and $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) = a_j$$$.

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Compare $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) \cdot (j - i)^2$$$ with $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_k) \cdot (k - i)^2 + \min(a_k \ldots a_j) \cdot (j - k)^2$$$ for some $$$i \lt k \lt j$$$.

hints.title: Hint 5
hints.content: Split $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) \lt \sqrt{n}$$$ into two more cases: $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) = a_i$$$ and $$$\min(a_i \ldots a_j) = a_j$$$.
